top_signals.title: China steps up claims over sea east of Taiwan - Financial Times
top_signals.summary: China steps up claims over sea east of Taiwan  Financial Times
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ESMA warns many prediction market event contracts already face EU retail ban
top_signals.summary: The European regulator said companies cannot circumvent EU financial rules by marketing binary-style products as event contracts rather than derivatives.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Spyware found on phone of European Parliament member probing it
top_signals.summary: Stelios Kouloglou, formerly a member of the European Parliament's committee investigating abuses of commercial spyware, was twice infected with Pegasus while serving, researchers said.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Ebola Vaccine Trials Could Start This Year
top_signals.summary: As Ebola cases rise in Congo and Uganda, vaccine developers are accelerating efforts to combat the rare Bundibugyo strain. IAVI CEO Mark Feinberg says a candidate vaccine could enter human trials by year-end. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Colombia's Oil and Gas Reserves Keep Shrinking
top_signals.summary: For a decade, Colombia's economically vital oil industry has been caught in a death spiral. A combination of sharply weaker oil prices, rising geopolitical risk and anti-petroleum industry reforms implemented by Colombia's first leftist president Gustavo Petro deterred investment, causing the operational tempo to decline. The main issue weighing heavily on the oil patch's outlook is a dire lack of proven petroleum reserves. These are the result of a lack of spending on wildcat drilling and a poor exploration success rate. After a lengthy delay,…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: FSCS Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements 2025/26
top_signals.summary: In its Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements published today (Thursday 2 July, 2026), the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) outlines the vital work it has carried out during the past financial year.

top_signals.title: US law enforcement group drops opposition to CLARITY Act: Report
top_signals.summary: The Major County Sheriffs of America said it still wants the CLARITY Act amended to give local law enforcement more resources to investigate illicit finance cases.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Why US exceptionalism in markets is justified
top_signals.summary: An honest conclusion is that America’s centrality rests on a set of mutually reinforcing advantages

top_signals.title: Shell alleged to have misled UK courts in Nigeria pollution case
top_signals.summary: Long-running trial over blighting of Niger Delta due to begin next year

top_signals.title: Industrial sector still lags on Asian representation, says Lee
U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text

top_signals.title: Worse Than an Axis
top_signals.summary: The informal alignment of U.S. adversaries is dangerous.

alerts.title: Europe Goes Its Own Way
alerts.summary: Drifting from America, the continent Is rearming and reordering itself.

alerts.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
alerts.summary: In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.
